spec_match.rationale: Row 1 uses abs() mathematically as a no-op since the scaled time value is always positive; however, Row 2 and 3 correctly implement folding and directional flipping.

interactive.rationale: Includes robust control buttons for play, pause, slow, and fast speeds, along with a live variable readout panel.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligns with the description of a linear sweep folded into bounce and indicator flip effects.

edge_cases.rationale: Does not include a support check banner or fallback indicators if the CSS @property api is unsupported.

references.rationale: Missing the required chromestatus.com/feature/5091423843778560 reference link.

design_system.rationale: Complies with standard design system styles and custom variable definitions.

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus reference link to Symmetry Animator concept page
openQuestions.detail: The concept page is missing the mandatory chromestatus.com/feature/5091423843778560 link in its references list.

openQuestions.title: Fix mathematical redundancy in Row 1 bounce calculation
openQuestions.detail: The horizontal animation `abs(var(--t) * 0.5 + 0.5)` is a no-op because the argument is always positive in the animated range [-1, 1]. To demonstrate actual abs() folding, use `abs(var(--t))` which maps [-1, 1] to [1, 0, 1], creating a true folded V-bounce.

openQuestions.title: Add capability support banner to Symmetry Animator
openQuestions.detail: The page lacks capability detection for `@property`, `abs()`, and `sign()`, unlike sibling pages. A banner should be added to warn users if their browser does not support CSS registered custom properties or sign-related math.

summary: An elegant keyframe animation demo showing absolute bounces and directional arrow flips. However, it contains a mathematical redundancy in Row 1 where abs() is a no-op, is missing a capability detection banner, and is missing the mandatory ChromeStatus reference link.
